subject: DevOps
title: Cross-Functional Teams
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Cross-Functional Teams.

hook.text: The dev team threw a ticket over the wall to Ops. Ops threw it back. Three weeks vanished in handoffs.

buildup.text: Siloed teams create handoffs. Each handoff adds delay, miscommunication, and lost context.

discovery.text: Cross-functional teams include dev, ops, QA, and security in one unit. They own the service end to end.

twist.text: Amazon calls it 'two-pizza teams.' Small enough to feed with two pizzas, empowered to own everything.

climax.text: When the team that builds it also runs it, they feel the pain of bad decisions and fix them fast.

punchline.text: You build it, you run it. Ownership changes everything.

quiz.question: What is Amazon's 'two-pizza team' concept?
options: Small cross-functional teams that own a service end to end
Teams that only meet during lunch breaks
A team building food delivery software
